info.title: Sensio API
info.description:
This is the API server for Sensio App.
For full documentation, visit /docs.

get.summary: [Common] Fetch device and booking info by MAC address
get.description: Fetches specific device information including booking_id, time_start, and time_stop directly from the Big API.

delete.description: Remove all data from the cache storage

post.description: Register a new device under a terminal

get.description: Retrieve a list of all device statuses
Retrieve all devices associated with a specific terminal
Retrieve device information by device ID

put.description: Update an existing device's details by ID
Update the status value for a specific device by ID

get.summary: [Terminal] [Device] Get device statuses by device ID
get.description: Retrieve all statuses for a specific device
Check if the application and database are healthy

post.summary: [Mail] Send an email using a template
post.description: Send an email using a server-side template and specified recipients

post.summary: [Mail] Send an email by Terminal MAC Address
post.description: Looks up customer email by MAC address and sends an email using a template

get.description: Get the status and result of an email sending task.

post.summary: [Models] Raw prompt query to Gemini model
post.description: Send a raw prompt directly to the Gemini LLM model without RAG orchestration.

post.summary: [Models] Raw prompt query to Groq model
post.description: Send a raw prompt directly to the Groq LLM model without RAG orchestration.

post.summary: [Models] Raw prompt query to local Llama.cpp model
post.description: Send a raw prompt directly to the local Llama.cpp LLM model without RAG orchestration.

post.summary: [Models] Raw prompt query to OpenAI model
post.description: Send a raw prompt directly to the OpenAI LLM model without RAG orchestration.

post.summary: [Models] Raw prompt query to Orion model
post.description: Send a raw prompt directly to the Orion LLM model without RAG orchestration.

post.summary: [Models] [Pipeline] Run unified AI pipeline (Transcribe -> Refine -> Translate -> Summarize)
post.description: Submits a single job that orchestrates multiple AI stages.

get.description: Poll for status and results of a unified pipeline job.

delete.description: Cancels an active pipeline task. Returns 200 if task was cancelled or already terminal, 404 if task not found.

post.description: Classifies user prompt into Chat or Control and returns appropriate response or redirection. Infrastructure failures (network, timeout, backend processing) return a graceful service-issue response, not an error.
Processes natural language device control commands.
Generate meeting minutes summary asynchronously. Returns a Task ID for polling.
Translate text to a target language asynchronously. Returns a Task ID for polling.
Execute a full AI pipeline (transcribe, translate, summarize)

get.description: Get the status of a pipeline job by ID

post.description: Chat using the legacy Python RAG service
Control devices using the legacy Python RAG service

get.description: Get the status of a RAG task by ID

post.description: Summarize text using the legacy Python RAG service
Translate text using the legacy Python RAG service

get.description: Get the status of a transcription task by ID

post.description: Transcribe an audio file by providing its local path
Initialize a new chunked upload session for a large audio file

get.description: Get the current status and progress of an upload session

put.description: Upload a single chunk of an audio file for a session

post.description: Start transcription of audio file using the configured LLM provider (LLM_PROVIDER). Asynchronous processing. Supports: .mp3, .wav, .m4a, .aac, .ogg, .flac.

get.description: Get the status and result of any transcription task (Short, Long, or Orion).
Get MQTT credentials by username for device authentication

post.summary: [Common] Publish a notification to all terminals in a room
post.description:
Computes publish_at = datetime_end (or time_end) - interval_time and immediately publishes it to all terminals in the room via MQTT.
At least one of datetime_end or time_end must be provided. If both are provided, datetime_end takes priority.

get.description: Get a paginated list of all recordings.

post.description: Upload an audio file and save its metadata.

get.description: Retrieve metadata for a specific recording.

delete.description: Remove a recording and its associated file from the system.

get.summary: [Scenes] List all scenes across all Terminal devices
get.description: Retrieve all configured scenes grouped under each Terminal device
Retrieve a list of all registered terminals with optional filtering

post.description: Register a new terminal device with MAC address and metadata

get.description: Retrieve terminal information by MAC address

get.summary: [Terminal] [Terminal] Get AI engine profile by MAC
get.description: Returns the AI engine profile (fast, standard) for a terminal identified by MAC address
Retrieve terminal information by terminal ID

put.description: Update an existing terminal record's metadata by ID

delete.description: Soft delete a terminal by ID

put.description: Sets or clears the AI engine profile (fast, standard) for a terminal. plaud is rejected as not yet available.

get.summary: [Scenes] [Terminal] List all scenes for a Terminal device
get.description: Retrieve a list of all configured scenes for a specific Terminal device, including all actions for each scene.

post.description: Create a scene with a name and a list of actions (Tuya/MQTT)

put.description: Update the configuration of a specific scene

get.description: Trigger all actions defined in a specific scene
Authenticates the user and retrieves a Tuya access token

Retrieves a list of all devices in a Merged View (Smart IR remotes merged with Hubs). Sorted alphabetically by Name. For infrared_ac devices, the status array is populated with saved device state (power, temp, mode, wind) or default values if no state exists.
Retrieves details of a specific device by its ID

post.description: Sends an infrared command (e.g., AC control) to an IR-enabled Tuya device.
Sends a standard switch command (e.g., toggle power) to a specific Tuya device.

get.description: Retrieves sensor data (temperature, humidity, etc.) for a specific device

post.description: Submit audio file for transcription via Gemini. Processing is asynchronous.
Submit audio file for transcription via Groq. Processing is asynchronous.
Submit audio file for transcription via OpenAI. Processing is asynchronous.
Submit audio file for transcription via Orion. Processing is asynchronous.
Submit audio file for transcription via Whisper.cpp. Processing is asynchronous.

pic.description: Person in charge (may be empty if not specified)

audio_class.description:
ASR Quality Gate Metadata (internal use for tuning and observability)
CRITICAL: These fields MUST always be present (no omitempty) for completed transcription results
to ensure frontend can reliably detect rejection states.

provider_skipped.description: ALWAYS present when audio analysis ran (no omitempty)

transcript_valid.description: ALWAYS present for completed results (no omitempty)

utterances.description: Optional structured artifacts (backward compatible - empty when not available)

owner.description: May be empty if not assigned

status.description: pending, processing, completed, failed, skipped, cancelled

request_id.description:
RequestID is a unique identifier for cross-channel idempotency.
When the same request_id is sent via both HTTP and MQTT channels,
only ONE Tuya command execution will occur. The second channel will
return either a "processing" acknowledgment (if first request is still
in progress) or the cached response (if first request completed).
Client should generate a UUID per user interaction and use it for both
HTTP and MQTT dispatch to prevent duplicate device control.

uid.description: Must be Tuya UID (never MAC/terminal identity)

text.example: Ini adalah transkrip panjang dari rapat teknis...

execution_result.description: holds the response from the fetched endpoint

summary.description: Alias for Result in summary tasks

summary_version.description: Optional structured summary artifacts (backward compatible - empty when not available)

text.example: This is a long transcript of a technical meeting...

utterances.description: Utterances within this segment, if available

end_ms.description: End time in milliseconds (ESTIMATE if provider doesn't supply)

start_ms.description: Start time in milliseconds (ESTIMATE if provider doesn't supply)

BearerAuth.description: Enter JWT token only (without "Bearer " prefix) - Swagger UI will add it automatically
